name: rn-native-features
description: Native iOS features in Expo React Native apps. Use when implementing camera, push notifications, haptics, permissions, device sensors, or other native APIs in Expo.
capabilities: Implement camera permissions and photo capture with CameraView
Set up push notifications with expo-notifications including token registration
Add haptic feedback using expo-haptics for UI interactions
Request and handle location permissions with expo-location
Store data securely using expo-secure-store for tokens and credentials
Manage app lifecycle and keyboard events in React Native
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: Documentation skill with TypeScript code examples for Expo React Native native features. Static scanner flagged Markdown code block delimiters as shell backticks (false positive) and Expo SDK APIs as cryptographic concerns (false positive). All code uses standard Expo modules with no actual security risks. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Building Expo React Native apps that need native iOS capabilities like camera, push notifications, haptics, and secure storage requires platform-specific patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use code examples for implementing these features correctly with proper permission handling and Expo best practices.
userTitle: Implement Native iOS Features in Expo React Native
limitations: Does not include native module development outside Expo SDK
Does not cover Android-specific native features
Does not include background task processing
Does not provide backend server implementation for push notifications

useCases.title: Build a Photo-Sharing App
useCases.description: Implement camera capture, photo preview, and gallery access with proper permission requests and user guidance.

useCases.targetUser: Product engineers implementing user notification systems
useCases.title: Create a Notification-Driven App
useCases.description: Set up push notification infrastructure for user engagement features like reminders, alerts, and real-time updates.

useCases.targetUser: Security-focused developers implementing secure credential storage
useCases.title: Secure User Authentication Storage
useCases.description: Store auth tokens securely using platform-native encryption via SecureStore for authentication flows.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Camera Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement camera permissions and take a photo in an Expo React Native app using CameraView. Include the permission request flow and handling of denied permissions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up camera functionality with proper permission handling

promptTemplates.title: Push Notification Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete push notification setup for Expo React Native including permission request, token registration, handling incoming notifications, and sending test notifications locally.

promptTemplates.title: Secure Token Storage
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I securely store authentication tokens in Expo React Native? Show me the difference between AsyncStorage and SecureStore and when to use each.

promptTemplates.title: Haptic Feedback Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me best practices for haptic feedback in Expo React Native. Include different impact styles, notification feedback types, and good patterns for button presses, form submissions, and error states.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding tactile feedback to user interactions

bestPractices: Always request permissions before accessing native features and handle the denied state gracefully with user guidance to Settings.
Use expo-secure-store for tokens and credentials, not AsyncStorage, to ensure platform-native encryption.
Check Device.isDevice before push notifications since they require a physical device, not a simulator.
antiPatterns: Storing sensitive data like API keys or auth tokens in AsyncStorage without encryption.
Skipping permission checks and directly accessing native APIs without user consent.
Implementing haptic feedback on every single tap without considering context and user experience.

faq.question: Does this skill work with plain React Native (without Expo)?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses Expo SDK modules like expo-camera, expo-notifications, and expo-secure-store. For plain React Native, you would need react-native-camera, react-native-push-notification, or react-native-keychain instead.

faq.question: Can I use these features in the iOS simulator?
faq.answer: Most native features require a physical device. Push notifications and haptics specifically do not work in simulators. Camera and location can work in simulators but behave differently than physical devices.

faq.question: What is the size limit for SecureStore?
faq.answer: SecureStore has a limit of approximately 2KB per item. Use it for tokens and small credentials only. For larger sensitive data, consider encryption libraries that split data into chunks.

faq.question: How do I handle permissions being permanently denied?
faq.answer: When permission.status is 'denied' and permission.canAskAgain is false, show a UI element with a button that calls Linking.openSettings() to direct users to manually enable permissions.

faq.question: Do I need a backend to send push notifications?
faq.answer: You can send notifications locally using scheduleNotificationAsync for testing. For production, you need a backend server to interact with Expo Push API and manage push tokens at scale.

faq.question: What accuracy levels are available for location?
faq.answer: Location.Accuracy options include Low, Lowest, Balanced, High, and Highest. Higher accuracy uses more battery. Balanced offers a good trade-off for most use cases.
